defense.critical_success: The target takes double damage and 1d4 persistent electricity damage.
defense.failure: The target takes the electricity damage, but not the slashing damage.

heightened.text: The slashing damage, initial electricity damage, and persistent electricity damage on a critical hit each increase by 1d4.

spell.name: Live Wire
spell.text: You conjure up a length of sharp copper filament humming with electrical current that strikes out at your foe. The wire deals 1d4 slashing damage and 1d4 electricity damage, depending on your spell attack roll against the target's AC.

traits.name: Attack
traits.text: An ability with this trait involves an attack. For each attack you make beyond the first on your turn, you take a multiple attack penalty.

traits.name: Cantrip
traits.text: A spell you can cast at will that is automatically heightened to half your level rounded up.

traits.name: Concentrate
traits.text: An action with this trait requires a degree of mental concentration and discipline.

traits.name: Electricity
traits.text: Effects with this trait deal electricity damage. A creature with this trait has a connection to magical electricity.

traits.name: Manipulate
traits.text: You must physically manipulate an item or make gestures to use an action with this trait. Creatures without a suitable appendage can’t perform actions with this trait. Manipulate actions often trigger reactions.

traits.name: Metal
traits.text:
Effects with the metal trait conjure or manipulate metal. Those that manipulate metal have no effect in an area without metal. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of metal or have a connection to magical metal.  
  
These planes consist of chaotic and shifting structures and oceans of metal. Metal planes tend to exist in a state of change and decay, leaving plentiful pockets of air for visitors to breathe or move within. Creatures unlucky enough to be entombed in the plane’s substance risk suffocation if they can’t phase through metal. Wood creatures find the lack of stability and soil on a metal plane disconcerting and often fail to thrive in such environments.
